decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the 85.0°C limit. The most effective immediate action is to increase the heat spreader conductivity, which is the primary lever for lateral heat spreading and has room to grow within bounds.

changes.reason: Maximizing spreader conductivity within the allowed single-step ratio (1.19x) enhances lateral heat distribution away from the chip hot spot, directly addressing the temperature violation.

expected_effects: Improve temperature uniformity across the spreader layer
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if the bottleneck shifts to the base plate conductivity or boundary conditions.
Further improvements might require geometric changes (spreader width/height) if material tuning saturates.
